aliases: Yellow Noodles in Spicy Potato Gravy

english: Yellow Noodles in Spicy Potato Gravy

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Mee Rebus; mapped to the broader 'noodle dish' class (FOODON_03311069) as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Noodle-based dish — composite mixed dish with noodles as primary ingredient
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Mee Rebus. A0FJP (composite noodle-based dish) is used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. Verified against EFSA FoodEx2 catalogue revision 2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Mee Rebus or equivalent Singaporean noodle dish as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Mee Rebus Special as served at a Singaporean hawker stall or food delivery outlet, weighing approximately 700 g and including noodles, gravy, toppings, chicken cutlet, and begedil.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore HPB Foodo — Mee Rebus / composite noodle dish nutrient profile; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual ingredient cross-validation
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; United States Department of Agriculture (USDA)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists Mee Rebus as a standard hawker dish. The input values (energy 150 kcal/100g, protein 6.43g, fat 7.86g, carbohydrate 13.57g) are consistent with HPB Foodo's published range for Mee Rebus (approximately 140–160 kcal/100g). Values are adopted from the input record and cross-validated against HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy component data. The 'Special' variant adds a fried chicken cutlet and begedil, which elevate fat and energy slightly above the standard version. Sodium 314 mg/100g is consistent with HPB data for gravy-based noodle dishes. All values retained from input as they fall within the defensible HPB range.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — yellow wheat noodles and potato-based composite dish analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mee Rebus Special. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on: (1) yellow wheat noodles GI ~52–58 (University of Sydney GI Database, boiled wheat noodles); (2) potato-based gravy component GI ~70–80 (boiled/mashed potato analogues); (3) composite dish weighting — noodles constitute approximately 50% of carbohydrate load, potato gravy approximately 35%, remaining from bean curd and vegetables. Weighted composite GI estimated at 65. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (95g from 700g × 13.57g/100g) ÷ 100 = 61.8. This is a high GL per serving due to the large 700g portion size.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 314 mg/100g; 2,200 mg per full 700g serving — approaching the 2,300 mg daily limit in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and salt in the gravy, limit portion size to approximately 350g, and avoid adding condiments at the table to keep sodium intake below 1,500 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL 61.8) driven by refined yellow wheat noodles and potato-based gravy, with low dietary fibre (1.14g/100g) insufficient to blunt postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to no more than 200–250g, request extra bean sprouts to increase fibre, omit or halve the begedil (fried potato patty), and monitor blood glucose response given the high estimated GI of 65 and very high per-serving glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 2.14g/100g and trans fat 0.07g/100g from deep-fried components (chicken cutlet, begedil); cholesterol 42.86mg/100g; sodium 314mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should request grilled or steamed chicken instead of the fried cutlet, omit the begedil to reduce saturated and trans fat intake, and keep the serving to 300g or less to limit total saturated fat to under 6g and sodium to under 1,000mg per meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried components generate advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; trans fat 0.07g/100g is pro-inflammatory; green chilli is a nightshade ingredient
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute the fried chicken cutlet with grilled or steamed chicken, omit the begedil, and monitor tolerance to green chilli as a nightshade; turmeric in the spice blend provides beneficial anti-inflammatory polyphenols and should be retained.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mee Rebus Special (特别马来卤面 / மீ ரெபுஸ் ஸ்பெஷல்) is a popular Singaporean-Malay hawker noodle dish consisting of yellow wheat noodles served in a thick, sweet-spicy potato-based gravy. The standard version is elevated in the 'Special' variant with the addition of a crispy deep-fried chicken cutlet and a begedil (fried potato patty). The dish is topped with cubed bean curd (tofu), fresh bean sprouts, a halved hard-boiled egg, green chilli slices, Chinese chives (kuchai), and whole shrimps. The gravy is made from mashed sweet potato and potato, blended with spices including turmeric, cumin, and dried shrimp, giving it a distinctive golden-orange colour, a thick viscous texture, and a balanced sweet-savoury-spicy flavour profile. The noodles are blanched, the gravy is simmered, and the chicken cutlet and begedil are deep-fried before assembly. Nutritionally, a 700g serving provides approximately 1,050 kcal, 45g protein, 55g fat, and 95g carbohydrate. The dish is high in sodium (~2,200mg per serving) and has a moderate GI (~65) but a very high glycemic load per serving (~61.8) due to the large portion size and refined carbohydrate content. Dietary fibre is low at 1.14g/100g. The dish is halal-certified in most hawker contexts. It is clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management (high GL, low fibre), hypertension (high sodium per serving), and cardiovascular disease (saturated and trans fat from deep-fried components). Mee Rebus is a culturally significant dish in Singapore and Malaysia, commonly eaten at breakfast or lunch at hawker centres and food courts.

confidence_reason: Input nutrient values are consistent with HPB Foodo published ranges for Mee Rebus (approximately 140–160 kcal/100g) and have been cross-validated against USDA SR Legacy component data. The 'Special' variant with chicken cutlet and begedil is not separately listed in HPB Foodo; values are adapted from the standard Mee Rebus entry with upward adjustment for the deep-fried additions. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Singaporean hawker dish is not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a single composite entry as of 2026. 2. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for Mee Rebus as a composite dish is not reported in HPB Foodo; individual ingredient estimates were not summed to avoid compounding estimation error. 3. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish is not reported in HPB Foodo. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient panel for this composite dish is not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a single composite entry as of 2026. 5. trans_fat_g 0.07g/100g is retained from input; this is consistent with trace trans fat from deep-frying in vegetable oil. 6. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on yellow wheat noodle and potato composite weighting; no direct GI measurement for Mee Rebus Special exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026. 7. The 'Special' variant adds a fried chicken cutlet and begedil not present in the standard Mee Rebus; nutrient values reflect this composite preparation. 8. Halal status is based on typical hawker preparation; individual vendor compliance should be verified.
